$comment: LawME Legal Knowledge Graph — relation (edge) type definitions. Every edge instance MUST carry the edge_metadata fields. Design only.

edge_metadata_required.confidence: 0.0-1.0 — extraction/inference confidence; 1.0 reserved for rule-derived or human-verified edges
edge_metadata_required.provenance: how the edge was created: citation_parser:<version> | human_review:<user> | licensed_metadata:<source_id> | inference:<model+version>
edge_metadata_required.evidence_anchor: paragraph/page anchor in the source document where the relation is asserted (null only for structural edges)
edge_metadata_required.verified: boolean — true only after human review or corroboration by a second independent extraction
edge_metadata_required.label: trust-model claim label: verified_primary | inference | unverified

relations.description: Document cites another authority
Inverse of cites — maintained automatically, never hand-written
Judgment interprets a statutory provision
Applies an existing rule to new facts
Distinguishes an earlier case on facts/law
Explicitly approves/adopts an earlier holding
Narrows an earlier holding
Overrules an earlier judgment — high-stakes edge, requires verification before surfacing as fact
Follows binding/persuasive precedent
Incompatible holdings/guidance — feeds contradiction surfacing
This case is an appeal of another
Inverse of appeals — derived
Amending act → amended provision
Repeal relation
Version chain: this version is effective from date X, superseding the target version
Weak association (same issue cluster, same parties…) — never used for authority
Case/document filed in court
Authored/decided by judge(s)
Representation in a specific case (edge carries case ref)
New legal development is relevant to a firm matter — drives notifications; firm-private, RLS-guarded
relations.primary_extraction: matcher: matter issue profile × document issues (inference-labeled until lawyer confirms)

relations.description: Authority supports a claim/argument
Authority against a claim — Red Team fuel
This artifact was produced from that source (summary→judgment, draft→template)
Claim/citation in the source was verified against the target primary authority (timestamped)
